items.title: The Worx Nitro 20V 8-inch cordless electric pole saw with 13.3-feet of reach is keeping to its $120 Prime Day rate
items.summary: Amazon is continuing its Prime Day pricing on the Worx Nitro 20V 8-inch Cordless Electric Pole Saw with 2.0Ah battery for $119.99 shipped. Normally climbing as high as $153 in 2026, discounts have dropped the costs over the last six months between $140 and $119, while there was a one-day deal in May to score it at the $110 low. The deal here began halfway through Prime Day and is continuing on for an unknown amount of time, giving you $33 savings and landing it just $10 above the low.

Marsail Ergonomic Drafting Chair

What we love

If you’re rocking a standing desk and want a chair that can handle taller heights, a drafting chair is certainly worth considering. This unit kicks fabric to the curb, leaving you with a wipeable surface that won’t stain very easily.

Normally going for $140 at full price, it held down at $115 from late April to Prime Day, when it fell to $76. Now, you can grab it for the next-best price with $40 savings, letting you expand your 24V battery arsenal for prolonged runtimes out in the yard this summer. You can also find the brand’s the brand’s tools in the official Amazon storefront here.

items.title: Elgato’s Facecam Neo USB-C webcam with privacy shutter is now lower than it was during Prime Day at just $30 (All-time low)
items.summary: Amazon is now offering Elgato’s Facecam Neo webcam for $29.99 shipped. This is a $100 model that has seen several discounts since launch, but it has mostly been sitting around the $50 mark in recent months. We saw it drop to $37.99 just last week during the Prime Day sale, and today’s offer takes another $8 off to set a new all-time low. That is a particularly great price for a webcam with features typically reserved for pricier options, so it is worth grabbing if you passed on the Prime Day deal.

items.title: SCOTUS finds that Trump's 2025 firing of FTC Commissioner Rebecca Kelly Slaughter without cause was lawful, placing independence of several agencies in doubt (NPR)
items.summary:
NPR:
SCOTUS finds that Trump's 2025 firing of FTC Commissioner Rebecca Kelly Slaughter without cause was lawful, placing independence of several agencies in doubt  —  The U.S. Supreme Court on Thursday further overturned a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies at will.

items.title: Anker’s extended SOLIX Prime Day Sale drops the 3,840Wh F3800 Plus power station to a new $1,985 low, more from $104
items.summary:
Update 6/29: Anker SOLIX has extended its Prime Day Sale event through the rest of June, giving you two more days to jump on some of the best prices we’ve seen this year!

Running parallel to Amazon’s Prime Day savings event that is in full swing, Anker SOLIX has launched its official complete Prime Day Sale lineup with increased discounts on power stations, bonus savings codes and FREE gifts. One of the biggest upsets during this sale is the 3,840Wh F3800 Plus Portable Power Station down at $1,985.50 shipped, after using the sitewide code USAFF5 at checkout. It’s dropping down from its new $2,499 MSRP since the brand made permanent price cuts in May (originally $4,799), which we’ve seen go as low as $2,199 since it hit the market in early 2025. Now, during this event, it’s not only starting off priced lower than ever, but with the bonus savings code you’re getting a combined $514 markdown off the new going rate ($2,814 off the original MSRP) for a new all-time low price. You can also find the alternate standard F3800 Portable Power Station down at a new all-time low of $1,519.05 shipped, after using the above code. Head below to browse the full lineup of deals while they last.

items.title: Let freedom roll on Lectric’s XP4 utility e-bikes + up to $473 in FREE gear from $999 for July 4th, more from $799
items.summary: Lectric has switched gears on its Better-than-Prime to its 4th of July e-bike Sale that is offering up to $694 in continuing savings on bundles. One of the models seeing increased savings, and comes as my favorite pick from the brand, is the XP4 750 Long-Range Utility e-bikes with $473 in FREE gear at $1,299 shipped, while the standard XP4 e-bikes get a $79 bundle at $999 shipped. These bundles are down from the $1,772 and $1,078 prices you’d be paying if the packages weren’t included for free, with the long-range models getting $49 more value than during Prime Day. You’ll be getting a LevelUp rack for the standard e-bikes, while the 750 models also get a suspension seat post, Elite headlight upgrade, a pair of mirrors, an accordion-style bike lock, a phone mount, and water-resistant pannier bags. Head below to browse the full lineup of deals while they last – plus, get my in-depth take on this e-bike from my hands-on review.

items.title: Save up to $2,500 in Dell’s Back to School sale: New XPS 13, Alienware gaming gear, more from $25
items.summary: Dell has kicked off its Back to School sale with fresh discounts across its PC lineup, including Alienware gaming gear, mainstream Dell laptops and desktops, XPS notebooks, monitors, and so on starting from $24.99 shipped. The event lands alongside Best Buy’s 4th of July sale, and you can save over $2,500 by cashing in some of these pricier machines. Notably, Dell is offering a FREE $50 store credit for Dell Rewards members on the new XPS 13, making it the very first chance to save on this brand-new thin and light laptop. Dell Rewards is free to join, and you can sign up here. Notably, you’ll also find deals on RTX 50-series Alienware gaming laptops and desktops, along with previous-generation X-series machines, and so on. This is shaping up to be one of Dell’s bigger sales of the year, making it a good time to see whether the system you have been eyeing has finally dropped in price. Head below for the best offers.

items.title: EcoFlow flash sale offers 2,048Wh DELTA 3 Max Plus power station + 10K power bank at $1,044, more from $1,234
items.summary: As part of EcoFlow’s extended Prime Day Sale that is continuing through July 3, the brand has launched a flash sale with up to 61% discounts on three units. Of those, we spied a return of the 2,048Wh DELTA 3 Max Plus Portable Power Station getting a FREE RAPID Pro 67W 3-in-1 10,000mAh power bank at $1,044.05 shipped, after using the code 26EFPDAFF at checkout, which beats out its Amazon pricing by $55. It goes for as much as $1,899 at full price, ($1,967 factoring in the value of the power bank), which we saw go as low as $1,049 in a similar flash sale earlier in June, while it’s only beaten out by a freak one-day price cut to $799 back in January. You’re getting the second-best price here for $923 total savings, with the deal only lasting for 48 more hours. Head below to browse the full flash sale lineup.
